metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'sidecar example' in AC3 (e.g., 'a linked review record in the sidecar JSONL, identified by ticket id') so the citing evidence is unambiguously evaluable.
evidence: 'a one-paragraph conclusion comment states whether the scorer discriminates…The conclusion comment cites at least two concrete sidecar examples by ticket id.'
ABSENCE: no definition or example of 'sidecar' appears anywhere in the plan body.
findings.finding: The term 'sidecar examples' in AC3 is undefined — an executor cannot know what artifact constitutes a valid 'sidecar example by ticket id,' making the criterion's measurability criterion unverifiable.
findings.location: AC3: '…cites at least two concrete sidecar examples by ticket id.'
scenarios: An executor writes a conclusion citing two reviews inline and believes AC3 is satisfied; a reviewer rejects it because 'sidecar' means an attached file artifact in the codebase convention — neither party can resolve the dispute from the plan text.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'cites at least two concrete sidecar examples by ticket id' with 'cites at least two specific review records from the 33-review corpus, identified by ticket id, showing their novelty score and priority score from the eval output.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `run_eval('plan-review-novelty')` invocation path with a mechanism that actually calls the production novelty sub-call (`_score_floor_novelty()` or equivalent in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py`) against each labeled case's `(finding, prior_finding)` pair — `eval_solver.run_case()` has no dispatch arm for `plan-review-novelty` and will raise `ValueError` for every case, making coverage 0% and never reaching the scorer.
evidence: In `eval_solver.run_case()`: `_criterion_id('plan-review-novelty', repo_root)` strips prefix to get `'novelty'`, checks `registry.by_id()` (contains rubric criterion IDs, not Pass-2 sub-call IDs), returns `None`.
`_code_review_prompt_id('plan-review-novelty')` returns `None` (no `code-review-` prefix).
None of the three hardcoded agentic IDs (`completion-verifier`, `ticket-quality`, `spec-alignment`) match, so `run_case()` raises `ValueError('no eval solver for prompt "plan-review-novelty"')`.
`run_eval()` catches this per `except Exception: out = None`, making coverage = 0/6 = 0.0 < the 0.8 `coverage_threshold`, so every epoch fails — never reaching the `discriminates_novelty` scorer.

The novelty sub-call in production is `_score_floor_novelty()` in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py` (lines 121–164), a separate op taking `(findings, prior_findings, plan, runner, cfg)` — not reachable from the eval harness's case dict.

Cached prior plan-review result in `.rebar/cache/plan-review/0730-6ceb-88c3-430c/be455c4caa756074074d.json` independently flagged this exact gap: 'eval_solver has no dispatch arm for the plan-review-novelty prompt'.
findings.finding: The live eval invocation path the plan specifies — `run_eval('plan-review-novelty')` via `eval_solver.run_case()` — has no dispatch arm for the `plan-review-novelty` prompt ID and will raise `ValueError` for every dataset case, producing 0% coverage and a `passed=False` result for a purely mechanical reason that has nothing to do with whether the scorer discriminates novelty.
findings.location: Plan body: 'the live run requires the `nava-rebar[eval]` extra + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and goes through the live eval runner (`run_eval()` in src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py, as invoked by the eval CI workflow)' and AC2: 'The LIVE eval runs to completion from the repo venv with the `[eval]` extra installed, via the live runner path (`run_eval()` / the eval CI invocation for the plan-review-novelty spec)'
scenarios: Operator follows AC2 literally: runs `run_eval('plan-review-novelty')` or its CLI equivalent; every case produces `ValueError`, all 6 outputs are `None`, coverage is 0.0, the eval reports `passed=False` with 0 cases scored — the scorer is never invoked, so the operator cannot distinguish a healthy from a defective scorer.

Operator interprets the AC2 gate pass/fail as evidence of scorer health: a `passed=False` result is ambiguous — it could mean the scorer failed OR the harness failed to dispatch — defeating the diagnostic purpose of the entire task.
findings.suggested_fix: For each labeled dataset case, construct a minimal invocation: treat `case['finding']` as the single current advisory finding and `case['prior_finding']` as the prior-review finding set, then call the novelty RunRequest directly (mirroring `_score_floor_novelty()` in `plan_review/__init__.py` lines 121–164) with the live runner. Capture the returned `novelty` scalar, compare against 0.5 vs. `case['kind']`, and record pass/fail. This tests the actual production path, not the eval harness dispatcher.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the prerequisite statement: the live eval runner path (`run_eval()` via `_live_solver()`) requires the `agents` extra (pydantic_ai), not the `eval` extra (Inspect AI); update the plan body and AC2 to state `nava-rebar[agents]` as the required install.
evidence: `_live_solver()` in `eval.py` line 562 imports from `rebar.llm.config import LLMConfig` and `rebar.llm.runner import get_runner`, then calls `live.preflight()` — gated by `OptionalDependencyError` from the `agents` extra check, NOT the `eval` extra.

`eval.py` docstring (line 21): 'only the actual run (:func:`run_eval`) imports Inspect AI, lazily, behind ``guard_import``' — but `_live_solver()` (the default solver for the non-Inspect path) needs `agents`, not `eval`.
`_llm_eval_commands._llm_setup()` distinguishes the `agents`, `eval`, and `tracing` extras as separate checks (line 227), confirming they are distinct.

`eval.py` comment at line 550–558: 'NOT routed through Inspect AI: our model call is a whole tool-using agentic op... A native loop over the reviewer's REAL op (via eval_solver)' — confirming the native run_eval path uses `agents`, not `eval`.
findings.finding: The plan states the live eval run 'requires the `nava-rebar[eval]` extra' but `run_eval()` / `_live_solver()` actually requires the `agents` extra (pydantic_ai); the `eval` extra is for Inspect AI, which `run_eval()` explicitly does NOT use.
findings.location: Plan body: 'the live run requires the `nava-rebar[eval]` extra + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY' and AC2: 'The LIVE eval runs to completion from the repo venv with the `[eval]` extra installed, via the live runner path'
scenarios: Operator installs only `nava-rebar[eval]` (Inspect AI) as instructed, then runs `run_eval()` — the `agents` extra (pydantic_ai) is absent, `_live_solver().preflight()` raises `OptionalDependencyError`, and every case fails before any model call.
The incorrect prerequisite causes the operator to mis-diagnose a dependency error as a scorer defect.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'requires the `nava-rebar[eval]` extra' with 'requires the `nava-rebar[agents]` extra (pydantic_ai — the live runner depends on this, not on Inspect AI)' in both the plan body and AC2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the required extra in the plan body and AC2: the live `run_eval()` path needs `nava-rebar[agents]` (pydantic_ai), not `nava-rebar[eval]` (Inspect AI), as confirmed by `_live_solver`'s own docstring in eval.py.
evidence: eval.py lines 551-554: 'A native loop over the reviewer's REAL op (via eval_solver) ... NOT routed through Inspect AI'
eval.py lines 562-564: '_live_solver ... Needs the agents extra (pydantic_ai)'
README.md line 220: 'pip install nava-rebar[eval]  # + prompt evaluation: rebar prompt eval (Inspect AI)' — the [eval] extra is for the offline spec-validator command, not run_eval()
findings.finding: The plan asserts the live eval run requires installing the `nava-rebar[eval]` extra, but `run_eval()` in eval.py is explicitly NOT routed through Inspect AI; its `_live_solver` needs the `agents` extra (pydantic_ai), not `[eval]`.
findings.location: Plan body: 'the live run requires the nava-rebar[eval] extra + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'; AC2: 'via the live eval runner (run_eval()... nava-rebar[eval] extra installed'
scenarios: Executor installs `[eval]` instead of `[agents]`, finds run_eval() still fails with a missing-pydantic_ai error, and cannot reproduce the live run as specified.
AC2's oracle is unsatisfiable as written if the executor strictly follows the stated dependency.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'nava-rebar[eval] extra' with 'nava-rebar[agents] extra' in both the plan body and AC2; `[eval]` is the offline spec-validation extra for `rebar prompt eval`, not for `run_eval()`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a context note acknowledging the 2026-07-11 operator decision that activated the rising floor unconditionally 'in lieu of the discriminates_novelty eval' (src/rebar/_config_schema.py:275), and clarify what new action the eval outcome is expected to drive now that the floor has no config off-switch.
evidence: src/rebar/_config_schema.py:275: 'operator-authorized on field evidence, 2026-07-11, in lieu of 150b's discriminates_novelty eval'

src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:181: 'The rising floor is always active (operator-authorized on field evidence, 2026-07-11 — in lieu of 150b's discriminates_novelty eval; the off switch was retired in story 4cdf)'

docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md:58-60: 'both flags now default ON — operator-authorized on field evidence in lieu of the discriminates_novelty eval; an explicit false on either is the back-out'
ABSENCE: the plan mentions neither this prior decision nor what new action the eval outcome would drive given the floor is already unconditionally on
findings.finding: The plan does not acknowledge the existing operator decision (2026-07-11) to activate the rising floor unconditionally 'in lieu of the discriminates_novelty eval,' which means the eval outcome can no longer drive the expected decision branch (activate/don't activate the floor) — the floor is already always-on with no config off-switch.
findings.location: Plan body: 'determine which, with evidence, and files the follow-up'; AC4: 'if healthy, this ticket's conclusion says so and no bug is filed'
scenarios: Executor completes the eval, finds the scorer is healthy, writes AC3/AC4 conclusion — but the conclusion has no actionable consequence because the activation decision was already made without the eval.
Executor finds the scorer is defective (AC4 bug-ticket branch) — but the floor is already always-on regardless, so the bug ticket's remediation path is unclear.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a solver dispatch arm in `eval_solver.run_case()` for `prompt_id == 'plan-review-novelty'` that invokes the novelty sub-call over the case's `prior_finding` + `finding` fields (mirroring `score_novelty` / `run_chunk` in `verify.py`), or specify the exact alternative invocation path the operator should use to run the live eval to completion with per-case verdicts.
evidence: eval_solver.run_case() line 228: `raise ValueError(f"no eval solver for prompt {prompt_id!r}")` — reached when prompt_id is 'plan-review-novelty' (not a criterion, not code-review-, not one of the three agentic ids)
_criterion_id('plan-review-novelty', repo_root) checks registry.by_id() — 'novelty' is PASS_NOVELTY (passes.py:226), a Pass-2 sub-call, not a plan-review criterion; returns None
Dataset cases carry fields: prior_finding, finding, kind, pair, expect, note — no 'input' key; the criterion arm's _run_criterion_case reads case.get('input') which would be empty even if dispatched
run_eval() per-case except block (eval.py:665): `out = None` + a failed junit entry — the case is unscored; coverage drops to 0; epoch_ok = False across all 3 epochs
findings.finding: The live eval runner (run_eval → eval_solver.run_case) has no dispatch arm for prompt_id 'plan-review-novelty': it is not a criterion (not in the plan-review registry), not a code-review prompt, and not one of the three hardcoded agentic reviewers ('completion-verifier', 'ticket-quality', 'spec-alignment'). run_case() will raise ValueError, run_eval()'s per-case except clause catches it, marks the case unscored (out=None), and the epoch fails coverage — producing zero discriminates_novelty verdicts. The eval spec's dataset cases also carry no 'input' field (they have 'prior_finding', 'finding', 'kind', 'pair'), so even a hypothetical criterion arm would fail. This is a proven runtime failure path: the live eval will produce 0 scored cases and the plan's AC2 cannot be satisfied.
findings.location: AC2 ('The LIVE eval runs to completion …'); src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py run_case() final raise ValueError; src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-novelty.eval.yaml dataset
scenarios: Operator follows AC2 exactly, invokes run_eval('plan-review-novelty'), all 6 dataset cases raise ValueError, coverage = 0/6 < coverage_threshold 0.8, all epochs fail, junit shows 6 run-error entries and 0 discriminates_novelty verdicts — plan's diagnostic is inoperative.

The plan is trying to determine whether the scorer silently fails; ironically the live eval itself silently fails for the same structural reason (no solver arm) rather than producing discriminating verdicts.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a co-located decision rule to AC3 specifying what eval outcome (e.g., 'discriminates_novelty passes on all 6 cases in ≥2 of 3 epochs') constitutes 'healthy', and what constitutes 'defective', and how the synthetic eval result is connected to the 33-case field observation (e.g., confirming whether the fail-safe 0.0 path is exercised in production runs).
evidence: AC3 text: 'a one-paragraph conclusion comment states whether the scorer discriminates (and drops were correctly withheld) or the scorer is defective' — no threshold or rule is given for which eval outcome maps to which conclusion

The eval spec discriminates_novelty scorer uses _NOVELTY_MIDPOINT = 0.5 (eval_scorers.py:335); the runtime uses t_novel=0.7 (config default); the plan never specifies how the gate result (e.g. 'all 6 cases pass') maps to 'healthy precision-first quadrant' vs 'defective'
The plan body says 'consistent either with a healthy precision-first quadrant … OR with a silently failing scorer' — two hypotheses, no decision rule distinguishing them from the eval output
findings.finding: AC3 requires a conclusion distinguishing 'scorer discriminates (drops correctly withheld)' from 'scorer is defective', but the plan provides no co-located decision rule specifying what eval outcome constitutes evidence for each branch. The eval's discriminates_novelty scorer uses a 0.5 per-case midpoint; the runtime rising floor uses T_novel=0.7 and priority < floor; neither threshold nor their relationship to the field observation ('33 eligible reviews, zero drops') is operationalized in the plan as a pass/fail criterion for the conclusion. An executor writing the AC3 paragraph has no specified evidentiary standard to ground 'healthy' vs 'defective'.
findings.location: AC3 ('The 33 eligible-but-zero-drop reviews are re-examined against the eval outcome …')
scenarios: Eval passes (discriminates_novelty scores all 6 cases correctly): does that prove 'healthy' given the 33-case field observation? The plan gives no argument connecting the synthetic eval result to the field data.
Eval partially passes (e.g. 4/6 cases correct, gate at_least(2) of 3 epochs passes): operator has no specified rule for whether this is 'defective' or 'boundary healthy'.

findings.finding: The '33 remediation-eligible reviews' are referenced with the definite article as a known set, but the plan provides no locating reference (no query, no file path, no prior ticket ID, no date-range filter) that would let an executor identify which 33 reviews to examine.
findings.location: AC2: 'The 33 eligible-but-zero-drop reviews are re-examined against the eval outcome'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a locating note such as: 'The 33 eligible reviews are the remediation-eligible reviews logged since the flip date; retrieve them with `rebar reviews list --status remediation-eligible --after <flip-date>` or by referencing the dataset at <path/ticket>.'

findings.finding: The plan's conclusion-writing step (AC2) must distinguish 'scorer is defective' from 'healthy precision-first quadrant', but the plan provides no co-located decision rule tying the eval output (per-case pass/fail at the 0.5 midpoint) to the runtime drop behavior (T_novel=0.7 threshold). The two fail-safe paths the plan alludes to — runtime `decide.novelty()` returning 0.0 (carryover, never dropped) vs. the eval scorer returning `ScoreResult(passed=False)` when the output lacks a `novelty` field — have different semantics, yet the plan collapses them under 'the fail-safe returns zero drops on error' without specifying which path corresponds to 'defective scorer' vs 'healthy scorer that never fired'.
findings.location: Plan body (second paragraph) and AC2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm and document how eval_solver.run_case dispatches 'plan-review-novelty' cases (fields: finding, prior_finding, kind) — or specify the bespoke solve= injection / alternative runner call that AC2 requires, since no existing eval_solver arm handles this sub-call prompt's data shape.
evidence: eval_solver.run_case strips 'plan-review-' prefix → 'novelty'; checks registry.by_id() — 'novelty' is not a rubric criterion id (A1, G1..G7, T1..T14, etc.); _criterion_id returns None (eval_solver.py:66-76)
_code_review_prompt_id('plan-review-novelty') returns None (not 'code-review-*')

Falls through to case_store block; prompt_id matches none of 'completion-verifier', 'ticket-quality', 'spec-alignment'; raises ValueError('no eval solver for prompt plan-review-novelty') (eval_solver.py:228)
The novelty dataset cases carry fields 'finding', 'prior_finding', 'kind' (plan-review-novelty.eval.yaml lines 35-74); none of the existing arms consume these fields
ABSENCE: no eval_solver arm for sub-call prompts (novelty, verifier) exists in the codebase
findings.finding: The live eval invocation path (run_eval() → _live_solver → eval_solver.run_case('plan-review-novelty', case)) will raise ValueError at runtime: eval_solver has no dispatch arm for the 'plan-review-novelty' sub-call prompt. It is not a plan-review criterion (registry.by_id() does not contain 'novelty'), not a code-review prompt, and not one of the three hardcoded agentic ids. The per-case data shape (fields: finding, prior_finding, kind) is also incompatible with every existing arm. The plan's AC2 would fail before a single case is scored.
findings.location: AC2 — 'The LIVE eval runs to completion … via the live runner path (run_eval() / the eval CI invocation for the plan-review-novelty spec)'
scenarios: Operator runs the live eval per AC2; run_eval() calls _live_solver which calls eval_solver.run_case; every case raises ValueError; all cases show as 'run error' in junit output; coverage drops to 0; gate fails — but for infra reasons, not a valid scorer verdict, making the diagnostic inconclusive.
If the operator uses a bespoke solve= injection instead, AC2's 'exact invocation' and 'live runner path' language becomes imprecise, and the plan needs to specify what that injection is.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `eval_solver.run_case` can dispatch `plan-review-novelty` cases, or add a solver arm for the novelty sub-call prompt before claiming the live run will 'run to completion'.
evidence: eval_solver.py lines 178-228: run_case dispatches to criterion arm (_criterion_id check), code-review arm (_code_review_prompt_id check), then three hardcoded agentic ids; falls through to `raise ValueError(f'no eval solver for prompt {prompt_id!r}')`
eval.py lines 663-675: the ValueError is caught by `except Exception` (BLE001), sets `out=None`, records a run-error JUnit case
plan-review-novelty is NOT in the criterion registry (eval_solver._criterion_id returns None for it — it starts with 'plan-review-' but strips to 'novelty', which is not a registered criterion id)
The eval spec dataset cases use keys `prior_finding`/`finding`/`kind`/`pair` — none of the three agentic solver branches consume those keys
ABSENCE: no `plan-review-novelty` arm exists in eval_solver.run_case
findings.finding: The live `run_eval('plan-review-novelty', ...)` call will crash on every case because `eval_solver.run_case` has no handler for the `plan-review-novelty` prompt_id: it is not a plan-review criterion (not in the criterion registry), not a code-review prefix, and not one of the three agentic prompt ids (`completion-verifier`, `ticket-quality`, `spec-alignment`). The BLE001 catch in `run_eval` records each case as unscored (`out=None`), coverage falls to 0, and the run reports FAIL without any `discriminates_novelty` verdicts — the very signal the plan set out to validate.
findings.location: AC2 — 'The LIVE eval runs to completion … via the live runner path (run_eval() / the eval CI invocation for the plan-review-novelty spec)'
scenarios: Operator follows the plan, invokes run_eval('plan-review-novelty'), gets 6 cases all erroring out (ValueError), coverage=0, gate=FAIL, no discriminates_novelty verdicts emitted — the entire diagnostic purpose of the task is defeated
Operator might mistake the coverage-failure for the 'silently failing scorer' hypothesis, drawing a false conclusion
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `plan-review-novelty` arm in `eval_solver.run_case` that calls the novelty sub-call directly (analogous to `_run_criterion_case` but feeding `prior_finding`+`finding` from the case dict), OR use the injectable `solve` parameter of `run_eval` to supply a bespoke solver for this diagnostic task rather than relying on the default `_live_solver`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the extra requirement in the plan to `nava-rebar[agents,eval]` (matching eval.yml line 100), since `run_eval`'s live solver needs `[agents]` (pydantic-ai) and the plan's stated `[eval]`-only install will fail at preflight.
evidence: eval.py lines 562-575 (_live_solver): imports `get_runner` from `rebar.llm.runner` and calls `live.preflight()`, which raises `OptionalDependencyError` when `[agents]` (pydantic_ai) is missing
eval.py lines 550-558: comment explicitly states run_eval is 'NOT routed through Inspect AI'
_optional.py line 34: `'eval': ('inspect_ai', ...)` — the [eval] extra installs inspect_ai, not pydantic_ai
eval.yml line 100: `python -m pip install -e '.[agents,eval]'` — CI installs BOTH extras
ABSENCE: plan text names only `[eval]`, never `[agents]`
findings.finding: The plan states the live run requires the `nava-rebar[eval]` extra, but the actual live runner (`_live_solver` inside `run_eval`) needs the `[agents]` extra (pydantic-ai / `get_runner` / `PydanticAIRunner`). The `[eval]` extra maps to `inspect_ai` which `run_eval` explicitly does NOT use (eval.py line 553: 'NOT routed through Inspect AI'). Without `[agents]`, `live.preflight()` raises `OptionalDependencyError` and the run fails immediately. The CI workflow installs both `[agents,eval]` (eval.yml line 100).
findings.location: Plan body — 'the live run requires the nava-rebar[eval] extra + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'
scenarios: Operator installs only `nava-rebar[eval]` as the plan says, runs the live eval, gets `OptionalDependencyError: install nava-rebar[agents]` immediately — cannot proceed without re-reading CI source
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the `nava-rebar[eval]` extra' with 'the `nava-rebar[agents,eval]` extras' in the plan body and AC2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the G7 containment gap: either (a) amend the parent epic's Success Criteria to exempt investigation-only spike tickets from the 'change landed on main via Gerrit' requirement, or (b) scope this leaf to include a concrete code/config fix that lands on main (e.g., a scorer fix if defective, or a config change enabling floor eligibility), so the leaf's deliverable is a subset of — not a contradiction of — the parent's AC2.
evidence: Leaf file_impact: [{'path': 'src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_novelty.md', 'reason': 'read-only: eval target, no edit planned'}]
Leaf verify_commands: [] (empty)
Leaf ACs are all [operator-attested] comment deliverables; AC4 produces a bug ticket at most
Parent Success Criteria: 'The six child tickets below are closed with their changes landed on main through Gerrit (LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1 per change)'
Parent AC2: 'Every child's change landed on main via Gerrit with both gate votes at +1'
Parent comment: 'All six children now proceed in order: 67da, d156, 8086, 5e64, 0730, a850' — explicitly counts 0730 as one of the six requiring a landed change
findings.finding: The leaf's plan delivers no landed code — all outputs are ticket comments and a potential follow-up ticket, with explicitly read-only file_impact and empty verify_commands — directly contradicting the parent epic's Success Criteria ('The six child tickets below are closed with their changes landed on main through Gerrit') and AC2 ('Every child's change landed on main via Gerrit with both gate votes at +1').
findings.location: Leaf file_impact / verify_commands; Parent Success Criteria ('changes landed on main through Gerrit') and Parent AC2
scenarios: The leaf closes after posting comment conclusions and filing a follow-up ticket — satisfying all its own ACs — but no Gerrit change exists, so the parent's AC2 can never be satisfied for this child without reopening the leaf or adding a separate implementation ticket.
The parent's 'all children closed with changes landed on main' gate check fails at epic closure time because the 0730 child has no associated Gerrit CL, blocking the epic from closing.
findings.suggested_fix: If this ticket is intentionally a pure spike, update the parent epic's Success Criteria to read: 'The six child tickets below are closed; implementation children must have their changes landed on main via Gerrit (LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1); investigation-only children (spikes) are exempt and instead require a follow-up ticket filed and linked.' Then re-review the parent. Alternatively, add a concrete outcome to this leaf — e.g., 'If defective, the bug fix CL is submitted as part of this task' — so a Gerrit change is produced.

findings.finding: The leaf is a pure investigative spike (all deliverables are ticket comments; file_impact is read-only; no verify_commands) but the parent epic's Acceptance Criteria explicitly require every child's change to land on main via Gerrit with LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1. The leaf cannot satisfy that parent AC, making it diverge from the parent's completion contract.
findings.location: Parent AC: 'Every child's change landed on main via Gerrit with both gate votes at +1' vs leaf file_impact 'read-only: eval target, no edit planned' and leaf verify_commands: []
findings.suggested_fix: Add a clause to the parent epic's AC such as: 'Investigative-spike children (no code change produced) satisfy this AC by closing with operator-attested ticket comments; the Gerrit +1 requirement applies only to children with code changes.' Alternatively, amend this leaf's AC3 so that if the scorer is defective, fixing it (not just filing a bug) is in scope here, producing a Gerrit-landable change.

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: `rebar prompt eval plan-review-novelty` is an offline spec-validation command that outputs spec metadata (epochs, gate, scorer names, gold-set size, prompt SHA) — it does NOT execute model calls, run the discriminates_novelty scorer, or emit per-case pass/fail verdicts. AC1 requires 'the score/verdict per case' but the named command cannot produce that output; the live model run needs `nava-rebar[eval]` extra + API credentials and a different invocation path (e.g. the eval CI workflow or `run_eval()` directly).
findings.location: AC1: '`rebar prompt eval plan-review-novelty` (or the documented equivalent runner surfaced by `rebar prompt eval --help`)' and 'The comment includes the exact command, the score/verdict per case'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC1 command with the live-run path: either (a) `rebar criteria eval plan-review-novelty` if that invocation triggers `run_eval()` for this spec, or (b) document the eval CI workflow dispatch (`workflow_dispatch` on `eval-plan-review-container.yml` or an equivalent) as the attested runner, with its JSON output attached. Also remove the '(or the documented equivalent runner surfaced by `rebar prompt eval --help`)' fallback, since `--help` only shows the offline path.

findings.finding: `rebar prompt eval plan-review-novelty` is an offline spec-validator, not a live-model runner; it will not produce 'score/verdict per case' as AC1 requires.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1: '`rebar prompt eval plan-review-novelty` (or the documented equivalent runner surfaced by `rebar prompt eval --help`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Update AC1 to cite the live eval runner (e.g. the `.github/workflows/eval-plan-review-container.yml`-style invocation or `rebar prompt eval plan-review-novelty --live` if that flag exists) and add a prerequisite note that the `[eval]` extra (`pip install 'nava-rebar[eval]'`) and an API key must be present. Drop the `--help` hedge since `--help` does not surface a live-run command.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Harmonize the eval name throughout: replace 'discriminates_novelty' in the task title (or add an explicit alias note) so it matches the canonical spec identifier 'plan-review-novelty' used in AC1 and the plan body.
evidence: Task title: 'Run discriminates_novelty eval; determine why zero floor drops'
AC1: '`rebar prompt eval plan-review-novelty` reports the spec valid'
Plan body: 'prompt src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_novelty.md'
The eval is referred to by three different identifiers: 'discriminates_novelty', 'plan-review-novelty', and 'plan_review_novelty'
findings.finding: The task title uses 'discriminates_novelty' while AC1 and the plan body consistently use 'plan-review-novelty' for the same eval spec — an implementer searching for the eval spec by the task name will not find it.
findings.location: Task title ('Run discriminates_novelty eval') vs AC1 ('rebar prompt eval plan-review-novelty') and plan body ('plan-review-novelty scorer')
scenarios: Implementer runs `rebar prompt eval discriminates_novelty` (following the task title), gets a 'not found' error, and is confused about which spec to use.
Operator attesting AC2 records 'discriminates_novelty' in the ticket comment, making the audit trail inconsistent with the actual spec name.
findings.suggested_fix: Rename the task to 'Run plan-review-novelty eval; determine why zero floor drops' or add a parenthetical in the title: 'Run discriminates_novelty eval (plan-review-novelty spec); determine why zero floor drops'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an oracle for the healthy branch of AC4, e.g., 'the conclusion comment explicitly states no bug is warranted and `rebar deps <this-id>` shows no discovered_from edges.'
evidence: The oracle 'rebar deps <this-id>' applies only to the defective branch.
ABSENCE: no oracle or proving check is stated for the healthy branch — 'no bug is filed' is an absence-of-action that cannot be affirmatively confirmed.
findings.finding: The healthy-branch outcome of AC4 ('if healthy, this ticket's conclusion says so and no bug is filed') has no positive proving command — absence of a filed bug is not verifiable by any stated command.
findings.location: AC4: 'if healthy, this ticket's conclusion says so and no bug is filed. Oracle: rebar deps <this-id> shows the discovered_from link in the defective case.'
scenarios: A reviewer cannot distinguish 'healthy conclusion correctly reached and no bug filed' from 'scorer is defective but executor forgot to file the bug' without a positive artifact.
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC4: 'If healthy, the conclusion comment explicitly states the scorer is functioning correctly; `rebar deps <this-id>` returns no discovered_from edges, confirming no bug was filed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete example invocation (or the exact Python call) for the live eval run so the executor has a starting point without reading the CI YAML.
evidence: 'the exact invocation and the full per-case score/verdict summary recorded as a comment on this ticket'
ABSENCE: no example CLI invocation or Python call signature is given in the plan; the executor must reverse-engineer it from the CI YAML.
findings.finding: The live-eval invocation command is not provided in the plan; the executor is told to discover it from 'run_eval() in src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py, as invoked by the eval CI workflow,' then record it — but has no stated default or example to act on without reading external files.
findings.location: AC2 and plan body: 'the live run requires…goes through the live eval runner (run_eval() in src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py, as invoked by the eval CI workflow)'
scenarios: An executor unsure whether to call run_eval() directly in Python or via a CLI wrapper ships the wrong invocation and the result is unverifiable.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan body: 'Example invocation: `python -c "from rebar.llm.evals.eval import run_eval; run_eval(\'plan-review-novelty\')"` (adjust as needed for your environment); verify against .github/workflows/eval-ci.yml for the authoritative form.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale explaining why the chosen invocation path fits this diagnostic task (e.g., 'we use `run_eval()` because it exercises the full harness end-to-end' OR 'we invoke `_score_floor_novelty()` directly because the eval harness has no dispatch arm for Pass-2 sub-calls').
evidence: The plan states the mechanism ('goes through the live eval runner (`run_eval()`)') but gives no rationale for why this path — rather than directly calling `_score_floor_novelty()` with the labeled cases — is the right diagnostic approach.
ABSENCE: no sentence of the form 'we use `run_eval()` because [reason]' appears anywhere in the plan.

The absence matters here because `run_eval()` and direct sub-call invocation are structurally different approaches (harness vs. production path) with meaningfully different properties for this diagnostic task.
findings.finding: The plan provides no positive rationale for why `run_eval()` (the eval harness dispatcher) was chosen over directly invoking the production novelty sub-call (`_score_floor_novelty()`) to diagnose scorer health — leaving the implementer unable to evaluate whether the chosen mechanism is correct for the goal.
findings.location: Plan body overall rationale; no section stating why `run_eval()` fits better than a direct sub-call invocation.
scenarios: An implementer who notices the eval_solver gap might try to 'fix' the harness instead of switching to the direct invocation approach, wasting effort on the wrong layer.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit AC language requiring the operator to verify the run processed all expected cases (e.g., confirm per-case count == N) and to re-run or note any API errors/dropped cases in the conclusion comment, so a partial run cannot be mistaken for a clean zero-drop result.
evidence: Plan states the live run 'requires the `nava-rebar[eval]` extra + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY' and uses `run_eval()` but says nothing about retry, backoff, or partial-run recovery
The task's own hypothesis is that the scorer 'silently fails' (fail-safe returns zero on error), yet the plan has no guard against `run_eval()` itself silently dropping cases on API error
AC #2 is [operator-attested] for completion but gives no guidance if the run terminates early due to rate-limit or timeout after N of 33 cases
findings.finding: The live eval run invokes `run_eval()` against the Anthropic API with no specification of error handling, retry behavior, or partial-result durability — leaving the plan unable to distinguish 'eval completed with zero drops' from 'eval failed mid-run and returned partial/zero results', which is precisely the ambiguity it is trying to resolve.
findings.location: AC #2 (live eval runs to completion) and plan body ('live run requires... ANTHROPIC_API_KEY... run_eval()')
scenarios: Anthropic API rate-limits mid-run; `run_eval()` raises and the operator records a partial result as 'complete', invalidating the 33-case conclusion
A transient 5xx during one case causes that case's verdict to be silently omitted, making a defective scorer appear discriminating
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC #2 to read: '…the comment includes the exact command or runner call, per-case results, the run timestamp, AND a confirmation that all N expected cases were scored (no cases omitted due to API error); any error or dropped case must be noted and the run re-attempted before filing the conclusion.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
